_about: Human-labeled retrieval ground truth for OCI #312. Queries are agent-shaped (the way Claude Code phrases a search_code call), NOT keyword-shaped. expected_files are labeled BLIND -- decided by reading the repo source, never by looking at what search returns (see FM-2 in the ADR). Paths are repo-root-relative and must match the file_path strings the index stores; the first run's per-query breakdown is the calibration step. repo_id defaults to OCI's own indexed repo (env OCI_EVAL_REPO_ID overrides per run).

queries.query: where is the JWT validated before a request reaches a route
queries.notes: auth middleware; JWT local decode + API-key fallback

queries.query: how does hybrid search combine BM25 keyword scores with semantic vector scores
queries.notes: RRF fusion of BM25 + semantic candidates

queries.query: where is Cohere reranking applied to the search candidates
queries.notes: rerank step gated on COHERE_API_KEY (pro tier)

queries.query: how are code embeddings created in batches for indexing
which API route handles the version 2 search request
queries.notes: POST /search/v2 route; the path the MCP search_code tool hits

queries.query: how is repository cloning made durable and recovered when an indexing job gets stuck
where are import dependency graphs extracted from the parsed AST
queries.notes: tree-sitter import graph extraction, singleton service

queries.query: how does the MCP server forward a search_code tool call to the backend API
where is the codebase DNA and architectural pattern detection implemented
how does file path filtering avoid matching adjacent sibling directories
